commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.review_summary:
This PR updates Simplified Chinese Control UI translations in `ui/src/i18n/locales/zh-CN.ts` for common UI, Dreaming, cron, usage, and language labels.

Reproducibility: not applicable. for the central request because this is a locale polish PR, not a reported runtime bug. The blocking issues are source-reproducible from the diff and the generated i18n workflow files.
commands.review_followup: The remaining blockers are narrow and mechanical: preserve the intended zh-CN wording through the generated i18n artifacts and fix one untranslated label.

target.title: i18n(zh-CN): improve Chinese translations for Control UI
target.body: Improve Chinese (Simplified) translations across the Control UI, covering common UI strings, agent tabs, dreaming mode messages, cron job forms, and usage metrics. Replace ambiguous or overly technical translations with clearer alternatives. Key changes: - Replace "Dreaming" with "梦境" for all Dreaming-related strings - Improve jitter/stagger related translations for clarity - Fix untranslated strings in agent tabs, notes, and stats sections - Localize Tailscale serve references - Standardize zh-CN language display names - Update common section values for consistency ## Summary - Problem: Many Control UI strings remained in English or used awkward translations when viewed in zh-CN locale - Solution: Rewrote 30+ translations with clearer, more natural Chinese alternatives; added translations for previously untranslated strings - What changed: Updated `ui/src/i18n/locales/zh-CN.ts` - What did NOT change: All other locale files, non-locale source code, functionality or behavior ## Motivation Chinese-speaking users of the Control UI frequently encountered English strings or confusing translations (e.g. "Dreaming" left untranslated, "抖动" for stagger/jitter which has a different meaning in Chinese). This PR improves the overall zh-CN user experience by replacing ambiguous or overly technical translations with clearer alternatives more natural for native speakers. ## Change Type (select all) - [ ] Bug fix - [ ] Feature - [ ] Refactor required for the fix - [ ] Docs - [ ] Security hardening - [x] Chore/infra ## Scope (select all touched areas) - [ ] Gateway / orchestration - [ ] Skills / tool execution - [ ] Auth / tokens - [ ] Memory / storage - [ ] Integrations - [ ] API / contracts - [x] UI / DX - [ ] CI/CD / infra ## Linked Issue/PR N/A ## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs) - **Behavior or issue addressed:** Chinese (Simplified) locale file had untranslated or poorly translated strings in the Control UI - **Real environment tested:** Local OpenClaw gateway (2026.5.20) with zh-CN locale - **Evidence after fix:** (attach one screenshot of the Control UI showing the new Chinese translations) - **What was not tested:** The locale is a build-time artifact — syntax validation confirms structural integrity ## Root Cause N/A — not a bug fix. ## Regression Test Plan N/A — not a bug fix. ## User-visible / Behavior Changes zh-CN Control UI users will see: - "Dreaming" → "梦境" throughout dreaming section - "Cron Jobs" → "定时任务" in tabs, stats, and form fields - "Tailscale serve" → "Tailscale 服务" - "Browser enabled" → "浏览器已启用" - "Tool profile" → "工具配置" - Improved stagger/jitter terminology for clarity - Previously untranslated strings in restart confirmation dialog, empty job states, and job form now in Chinese ## Security Impact - New permissions/capabilities? **No** - Secrets/tokens handling changed? **No** - New/changed network calls? **No** - Command/tool execution surface changed? **No** - Data access scope changed? **No** ## Human Verification - Verified scenarios: Compared each changed string against the English source (en.ts) to ensure accuracy - Edge cases checked: Template interpolation patterns ({count}, {value}, {time}) preserved correctly; braces balanced; TypeScript structure intact - What I did **not** verify: Visual rendering (locale is compiled by Vite at build time) ## Compatibility / Migration - Backward compatible? **Yes** - Config/env changes? **No** - Migration needed? **No** ## Risks and Mitigations None — locale data change only, no logic, no API surface, no new dependencies.
target.author: ydym233

commands.reason: repair commands require a ClawSweeper PR or a PR opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge
